definition: Sum of all open deal amounts regardless of stage or probability. Represents the gross opportunity the sales team is working on.
benchmark: Should be 3–5× your revenue target for the period
formula: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN ('Closed Won','Closed Lost')
insight_template: Total pipeline is {value}. Coverage ratio vs target is {coverage}x. Healthy range is 3–5x.

definition: Sum of deal amounts multiplied by their stage probability. A more realistic estimate of expected revenue from open pipeline.
benchmark: Should cover at least 1.5× revenue target
formula: SUM(amount × probability/100) WHERE stage NOT IN ('Closed Won','Closed Lost')
insight_template: Weighted pipeline is {value} vs unweighted {raw}. Discount factor is {pct}%.

definition: Ratio of open pipeline value to revenue target. Tells you how many dollars of pipeline exist for every dollar of quota.
benchmark: 3x for inside sales, 4–5x for enterprise
formula: Total Pipeline Value / Revenue Target
insight_template: Coverage is {value}x. Below 3x signals pipeline risk for the quarter.

definition: Number of new deals added to the pipeline in a given period. A leading indicator of future revenue.
benchmark: Track MoM growth; decline 2 months in a row is a red flag
formula: COUNT(deal_id) WHERE created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} deals created this period. MoM change is {mom}%.

definition: Number of deals successfully converted to customers in the period. Core output metric for sales teams.
benchmark: Benchmark against quota and prior period
formula: COUNT(deal_id) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won' AND close_date IN period
insight_template: {value} deals closed won. Avg deal size: {avg_size}.

definition: Number of deals that were lost to competition, budget, or no decision. Important to track alongside won for win rate.
benchmark: Track loss reasons; if >40% is 'No Decision' pipeline quality is low
formula: COUNT(deal_id) WHERE stage = 'Closed Lost' AND close_date IN period
insight_template: {value} deals lost. Top loss reason: {top_reason} at {pct}%.

definition: Mean value of closed won deals. Tracks whether you are moving upmarket or downmarket over time.
benchmark: SMB: $5K–$30K, Mid-Market: $30K–$150K, Enterprise: $100K+
formula: SUM(amount) / COUNT(deal_id) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Avg deal size is {value}. {direction} vs prior period by {pct}%.

definition: Middle value of deal amounts. Less skewed by outlier enterprise deals than average — better for understanding typical deal.
benchmark: Should be within 20% of average; large gap means outlier distortion
formula: MEDIAN(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Median deal is {value} vs avg {avg}. Gap of {gap} suggests outlier deals.

definition: Revenue generated per day from the pipeline. Combines win rate, deal size, deals in pipeline, and cycle length into one number.
benchmark: Higher is always better; track weekly for sales ops
formula: (Deals in Pipeline × Win Rate × Avg Deal Size) / Avg Sales Cycle Days
insight_template: Deal velocity is {value}/day. Bottleneck stage is {stage} with avg {days} days.

definition: Average number of days from deal creation to close won. Shorter cycles mean faster revenue; longer cycles tie up resources.
benchmark: SMB: 14–30d, Mid-Market: 30–90d, Enterprise: 60–180d
formula: AVG(sales_cycle_days) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Avg cycle is {value} days. {pct}% of deals close within 30 days.

definition: Percentage of deals that advance from one stage to the next. Reveals where deals are dying in the funnel.
benchmark: Demo-to-Proposal >50%, Proposal-to-Negotiation >60%
formula: COUNT(deals reaching stage N+1) / COUNT(deals entering stage N)
insight_template: Weakest conversion is {stage} at {pct}%. Industry avg is {benchmark}%.

definition: Distribution of deal count and value across each sales stage. Shows where deals are concentrated and where bottlenecks exist.
benchmark: Healthy pipeline has deals spread across all stages, not bunched at top
formula: GROUP BY stage: COUNT and SUM(amount)
insight_template: {stage} holds {pct}% of pipeline value ({value}).

definition: Each rep's open pipeline value. Identifies reps who are under-pipelined vs over-pipelined relative to quota.
benchmark: Each rep should carry 3–4× their quota in pipeline
formula: GROUP BY owner_rep: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: Top rep is {rep} with {value}. {n} reps are below 3x coverage.

definition: Breakdown of pipeline value by lead source. Shows which acquisition channels generate the most valuable pipeline.
benchmark: No single source should exceed 60% to avoid channel concentration risk
formula: GROUP BY source: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: Top source is {source} at {pct}%. {risk} concentration risk.

definition: Pipeline value distributed across target verticals. Helps identify which industries are most active and which need more penetration.
benchmark: Compare to ICP definition — top 3 industries should match target verticals
formula: GROUP BY industry: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: Top vertical is {industry} at {value}. {ind} underperforming vs ICP target.

definition: Geographic distribution of open pipeline. Used for territory planning, hiring, and regional go-to-market strategy.
benchmark: Should align with regional headcount and quota allocation
formula: GROUP BY region: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: {region} leads with {value}. {region2} is the fastest growing at {pct}% MoM.

definition: Open pipeline segmented by product. Shows which products are gaining traction and which are stalling in the sales process.
benchmark: Cross-sell products should show in pipeline within 6 months of launch
formula: GROUP BY product: SUM(amount) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: {product} leads pipeline at {value}. {product2} has lowest avg deal size.

definition: Average number of days deals have been sitting in the current stage. Old deals in early stages are a sign of stale pipeline.
benchmark: Any deal >2× the avg sales cycle with no close date is likely dead
formula: AVG(today - created_date) WHERE stage NOT IN closed
insight_template: Avg pipeline age is {value} days. {n} deals are older than {threshold} days.

definition: Deals that had a close date in the previous period but did not close. Slippage is a leading indicator of forecast inaccuracy.
benchmark: Slippage >15% of committed pipeline signals a forecast reliability issue
formula: COUNT(deals where close_date was in last period but stage is still open)
insight_template: {value} deals slipped from last period totaling {amount}.

definition: Open deals with no logged activity in the last 14+ days. Lack of activity is the strongest predictor of deal loss.
benchmark: No deal in Proposal or Negotiation should go 7+ days without activity
formula: COUNT(deal_id) WHERE stage NOT IN closed AND last_activity > 14 days ago
insight_template: {value} deals at risk worth {amount}. {rep} has most at-risk deals.

definition: Total value of contracts signed in the period (new ARR). Bookings is a forward-looking metric — revenue is recognized later.
benchmark: Track against ACV quota; 100%+ attainment is the goal
formula: SUM(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won' AND close_date IN period
insight_template: Bookings this period: {value}. {pct}% of quarterly target.

definition: Revenue actually earned and recognized per accounting standards. Lags bookings for SaaS (spread over contract period).
benchmark: Should grow in line with ARR; sudden spikes/dips signal billing issues
formula: SUM(net_amount) FROM invoices WHERE is_paid = True AND paid_date IN period
insight_template: Revenue recognized: {value}. Running {pct}% ahead/behind bookings.

definition: Annual Recurring Revenue — annualized value of all active subscription contracts. The north star metric for SaaS businesses.
benchmark: World-class SaaS companies grow ARR 100%+ (Rule of 40 applies)
formula: SUM(acv) FROM customers WHERE is_churned = False
insight_template: Current ARR: {value}. YoY growth: {yoy}%.

definition: Monthly Recurring Revenue — ARR divided by 12. Used for month-over-month tracking of subscription health.
benchmark: MoM growth of 10–15% = strong; below 5% = concern
formula: ARR / 12
insight_template: MRR: {value}. MoM growth: {mom}%.

definition: Month-over-month or year-over-year percentage change in ARR. Key health indicator for subscription businesses.
benchmark: Early stage: 15–20% MoM. Scale stage: 5–10% MoM
formula: (ARR current - ARR prior) / ARR prior × 100
insight_template: ARR growth rate: {value}% MoM. 3-month trend: {trend}.

definition: ARR from brand new customers acquired in the period. Excludes expansion or renewal. Pure new business metric.
benchmark: Should grow faster than churn to ensure net positive ARR movement
formula: SUM(acv) FROM customers WHERE acquisition_date IN period
insight_template: New ARR this period: {value} from {n} new customers.

definition: Additional ARR from existing customers via upsell, cross-sell, or seat expansion. Most efficient form of revenue growth.
benchmark: Best-in-class: Expansion ARR covers 30–40% of new ARR target
formula: SUM(amount) FROM deals WHERE is_upsell = True AND stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Expansion ARR: {value}. {pct}% of total new ARR comes from expansion.

definition: ARR lost from customers who cancelled or did not renew in the period. The single biggest threat to compounding SaaS growth.
benchmark: Annual churn below 5% is excellent; above 15% is critical
formula: SUM(acv) FROM customers WHERE churn_date IN period
insight_template: Churned ARR: {value}. {pct}% of beginning ARR. {n} customers lost.

definition: New ARR + Expansion ARR − Churned ARR. The definitive measure of ARR momentum in a single number.
benchmark: Must be positive to grow; negative means you are shrinking
formula: New ARR + Expansion ARR - Churned ARR
insight_template: Net New ARR: {value}. Churn is {churn_pct}% offsetting new business.

definition: Net Revenue Retention — revenue retained + expanded from cohort of customers vs same cohort a year ago. Includes expansion minus churn.
benchmark: >120% is world-class (expansion outpaces churn). Below 100% means shrinking
formula: (Beginning ARR + Expansion - Churn - Contraction) / Beginning ARR × 100
insight_template: NRR: {value}%. {status} — benchmark is 100%+.

definition: Gross Revenue Retention — retention without expansion credit. Shows baseline ability to retain revenue regardless of upsell.
benchmark: Above 85% is good, above 90% is excellent for B2B SaaS
formula: (Beginning ARR - Churn - Contraction) / Beginning ARR × 100
insight_template: GRR: {value}%. Pure retention without expansion credit.

definition: Closed won revenue split by product line. Identifies top revenue-generating products and growth opportunities.
benchmark: No single product should exceed 70% to reduce product concentration risk
formula: GROUP BY product: SUM(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: {product} drives {pct}% of revenue. {product2} is fastest growing.

definition: Closed won revenue split by geographic region. Guides territory investment decisions and hiring plans.
benchmark: Align with headcount ratio; revenue per head should be consistent
formula: GROUP BY region: SUM(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: {region} leads revenue at {value}. {region2} growing fastest at {pct}% QoQ.

definition: Revenue segmented by customer industry vertical. Reveals which verticals monetize best and which underperform.
benchmark: Top 3 verticals should match ICP definition
formula: GROUP BY industry: SUM(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: {industry} generates most revenue at {value}. Avg deal size by vertical varies by {range}.

definition: Actual closed won revenue compared to quota or revenue target for the period. The primary sales performance metric.
benchmark: 100% attainment is the floor; top companies average 105–110%
formula: SUM(amount WHERE stage='Closed Won') / Revenue Target × 100
insight_template: At {value}% of target. {ahead_behind} by {delta} with {days} days remaining.

definition: Closed won revenue attributed to each sales rep. Used for performance ranking, incentive calculation, and coaching prioritization.
benchmark: Top quartile generates 2× median rep revenue
formula: GROUP BY owner_rep: SUM(amount) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Top rep: {rep} at {value}. Bottom quartile avg: {bottom_avg}.

definition: Each rep's revenue as a percentage of their assigned quota. The ultimate sales rep KPI.
benchmark: 60–70% of reps at or above 100% is healthy; below 50% signals systemic issues
formula: Revenue per Rep / Rep Quota × 100
insight_template: {n} of {total} reps at quota. Top attainment: {top_rep} at {pct}%.

definition: Time taken for a new sales rep to reach full productivity (100% of their quota). Critical for headcount planning.
benchmark: SMB reps: 3–4 months. Enterprise reps: 6–9 months
formula: AVG(months from hire date to first month at >80% quota)
insight_template: Avg ramp time: {value} months. {n} reps still ramping.

definition: Projected revenue for the current quarter based on pipeline, historical win rates, and deal stage. Informs board reporting.
benchmark: Forecast accuracy within ±10% is excellent; ±20% is acceptable
formula: Closed + Commit Deals + (Best Case × 0.5) + (Pipeline × Win Rate)
insight_template: Forecast: {value}. Confidence range: {low} to {high}.

definition: How close the sales forecast was to actual revenue at quarter end. Measures quality of pipeline and rep judgment.
benchmark: >90% accuracy means excellent inspection culture and pipeline hygiene
formula: Actual Revenue / Forecast Revenue × 100
insight_template: Forecast accuracy last quarter: {value}%. Trend: {trend}.

definition: Total number of active paying customers at the end of the period. Core business size metric.
benchmark: Track MoM net addition (new minus churned)
formula: COUNT(customer_id) WHERE is_churned = False
insight_template: {value} active customers. Net addition this month: {net}.

definition: Count of new customers acquired in the period. Distinct from new ARR as a small logo may have a large ACV.
benchmark: Track alongside CAC — acquisition rate should justify cost
formula: COUNT(customer_id) WHERE acquisition_date IN period
insight_template: {value} new customers this period. Avg ACV: {avg_acv}.

definition: Count of customers who cancelled in the period. Also called logo churn. Each lost customer is a growth setback.
benchmark: Logo churn below 5% annually is healthy
formula: COUNT(customer_id) WHERE churn_date IN period
insight_template: {value} customers churned this period. Avg ACV lost: {avg_acv}.

definition: Percentage of customers lost in the period out of total active at start. Monthly or annual rate.
benchmark: Monthly: below 1%. Annual: below 5% is strong
formula: Churned Customers / Customers at Start of Period × 100
insight_template: Churn rate: {value}% this period. Annualized: {annual}%.

definition: Percentage of customers retained at end of period vs start. Inverse of logo churn rate.
benchmark: Above 95% annually is excellent for B2B
formula: (1 - Customer Churn Rate) × 100
insight_template: Logo retention: {value}%. {n} customers retained of {total}.

definition: Total revenue expected from a customer over their lifetime. Calculated as ACV × expected lifetime in years.
benchmark: CLV should be 3–5× CAC for a healthy unit economics model
formula: ACV × (1 / Annual Churn Rate)
insight_template: Avg CLV: {value}. CLV:CAC ratio: {ratio}x.

definition: Total sales + marketing spend divided by number of new customers. Measures efficiency of go-to-market.
benchmark: CAC should be recovered within 12–18 months via CLV
formula: (Sales Costs + Marketing Costs) / New Customers Acquired
insight_template: CAC: {value}. Trend: {trend} vs prior period.

definition: Months required to recover CAC from customer revenue. Shorter payback = more capital efficient growth.
benchmark: Under 12 months is good. Under 6 months is exceptional. Above 24 is a concern.
formula: CAC / (ACV / 12)
insight_template: CAC payback: {value} months. {status} vs 12-month benchmark.

definition: Ratio of lifetime value to acquisition cost. The single most important unit economics metric for SaaS.
benchmark: 3:1 is the minimum viable ratio. 5:1+ is excellent. Below 2:1 is unsustainable.
formula: CLV / CAC
insight_template: LTV:CAC = {value}:1. {status} — target is 3:1 minimum.

definition: Average annualized value of customer contracts. Used to segment customers and set sales motion (PLG vs high-touch).
benchmark: SMB ACV <$20K, Mid-Market $20–100K, Enterprise $100K+
formula: SUM(acv) / COUNT(customer_id) WHERE is_churned = False
insight_template: Avg ACV: {value}. Segment mix: SMB {smb}%, MM {mm}%, ENT {ent}%.

definition: Total value of a contract over its full term (multi-year). Different from ACV which normalizes to one year.
benchmark: Multi-year deals have higher TCV but may mask annual churn risk
formula: SUM(amount) FROM orders + deals WHERE is_churned = False
insight_template: Total TCV under management: {value}. Avg contract term: {months} months.

definition: Distribution of customers across SMB, Mid-Market, and Enterprise. Guides resource allocation and product priorities.
benchmark: Revenue-weighted mix should align with GTM strategy and headcount
formula: GROUP BY segment: COUNT(customer_id)
insight_template: {segment} is largest at {pct}% of logos but {rev_pct}% of ARR.

definition: Count of customers per industry vertical. Validates whether ICP targeting is translating to actual wins.
benchmark: Top 3 industries should represent 60–70% of customer base
formula: GROUP BY industry: COUNT(customer_id)
insight_template: Top industry: {industry} with {n} customers. Coverage across {total} verticals.

definition: Geographic distribution of customers. Used for support staffing, expansion planning, and localization priorities.
benchmark: Should match regional sales investment proportionally
formula: GROUP BY region: COUNT(customer_id)
insight_template: {region} has most customers at {n}. Fastest growing region: {growing}.

definition: Ratio of customers using the product actively vs those who are paying but disengaged. Strong churn predictor.
benchmark: Inactive rate above 20% is a warning sign; expect churn in 60–90 days
formula: COUNT(health_score < 40) vs COUNT(health_score >= 40)
insight_template: {inactive_pct}% of customers are inactive (health <40). {n} at churn risk.

definition: Total number of contact records in the CRM. Measures the size of addressable market the team is actively tracking.
benchmark: Quality > quantity; track data completeness alongside raw count
formula: COUNT(contact_id)
insight_template: {value} total contacts. Growth MoM: {mom}%.

definition: New contact records created in the period. Measures top-of-funnel growth and outbound/inbound activity.
benchmark: Should grow consistently with marketing spend and SDR activity
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} new contacts this period. MoM change: {mom}%.

definition: Distribution of contacts by acquisition channel. Shows which sources fill the top of funnel most efficiently.
benchmark: Organic + referral sources typically convert to customers 2× faster than paid
formula: GROUP BY source: COUNT(contact_id)
insight_template: Top source: {source} at {pct}%. Referral converting at {ref_rate}%.

definition: Count of contacts at each lifecycle stage from subscriber to evangelist. Shows funnel shape and conversion health.
benchmark: MQL:SQL ratio should be 5:1 or better (not all MQLs are good fits)
formula: GROUP BY lifecycle_stage: COUNT(contact_id)
insight_template: {stage} has {n} contacts. Conversion rate to next stage: {pct}%.

definition: Marketing Qualified Leads — contacts that meet criteria to be handed to sales. Volume is the primary marketing output metric.
benchmark: Track MoM volume and ensure quality doesn't drop as volume grows
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE lifecycle_stage = 'MQL' AND created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} MQLs this period. MoM: {mom}%. Source breakdown: {top_source}.

definition: Percentage of MQLs that pass sales qualification criteria and become SQLs. Measures marketing-to-sales handoff quality.
benchmark: 20–30% is typical. Below 10% means MQL definition is too loose.
formula: COUNT(SQL) / COUNT(MQL) × 100 for same cohort
insight_template: MQL→SQL rate: {value}%. {direction} vs prior period. Top converting source: {source}.

definition: Sales Qualified Leads confirmed by reps as meeting BANT or equivalent criteria. The handoff point to opportunity management.
benchmark: SQL volume × avg deal size should match pipeline creation targets
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE lifecycle_stage = 'SQL' AND created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} SQLs this period. Implied pipeline value: {pipeline}.

definition: Percentage of SQLs that become formal deal opportunities in the CRM. Measures rep follow-through on warm leads.
benchmark: Above 60% is strong; below 40% suggests reps are not working SQLs properly
formula: COUNT(Opportunities) / COUNT(SQLs) × 100
insight_template: SQL→Opportunity rate: {value}%. {n} SQLs with no deal created.

definition: Average hours between a lead being created/assigned and first rep contact. Speed to lead is one of the strongest conversion predictors.
benchmark: Respond within 5 minutes: 9× better conversion. Industry avg: 42 hours.
formula: AVG(first_activity_date - created_date) in hours
insight_template: Avg response time: {value} hours. {n} leads took >24h to contact.

definition: Percentage of leads that go cold (no activity for 30+ days) without converting. High decay = wasted top-of-funnel spend.
benchmark: Below 20% decay within 30 days is good. Above 40% means nurture program is failing.
formula: COUNT(contacts with no activity >30d and not in Customer/Opp stage) / Total Leads
insight_template: {value}% of leads have decayed this period. {n} contacts need re-engagement.

definition: Average number of contacts mapped to each target account. Multi-threading (3+ contacts) dramatically increases win rates.
benchmark: Enterprise deals with 4+ contacts win at 2× rate of single-contact deals
formula: COUNT(contact_id) / COUNT(DISTINCT company) from contacts
insight_template: Avg {value} contacts per account. {n} accounts have only 1 contact (at risk).

definition: Count of contacts with zero activity logged in the last 90 days. Indicates CRM hygiene issues or neglected territories.
benchmark: No contact in active pipeline stage should go 90+ days without activity
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE last_activity_date < 90 days ago
insight_template: {value} contacts dormant 90+ days. Represents {pct}% of total database.

definition: Contact record distribution across rep/owner assignments. Identifies reps who are over or under-loaded.
benchmark: Balanced assignment; no rep should have >3× the median contact count
formula: GROUP BY owner_rep: COUNT(contact_id)
insight_template: Most loaded: {rep} with {n} contacts. {n2} reps have under 100 contacts.

definition: Percentage of contacts who have opted out of marketing emails. High opt-out indicates poor email targeting or messaging.
benchmark: Below 2% is healthy. Above 5% impacts deliverability and domain reputation.
formula: COUNT(email_opt_out = True) / COUNT(contact_id) × 100
insight_template: Opt-out rate: {value}%. {n} contacts unreachable by email.

definition: Average percentage of required CRM fields filled per contact record. Poor data = poor targeting and reporting.
benchmark: Above 80% completeness for key fields (email, company, title, phone)
formula: AVG(data_complete_pct) across all contacts
insight_template: Avg data completeness: {value}%. {n} contacts below 60% threshold.

definition: Leads that came in via inbound channels (demo requests, content downloads, organic search). Highest intent leads.
benchmark: Inbound leads convert to customers at 3–5× rate of outbound
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE source IN ('Inbound Demo','Organic SEO','Paid Search') AND created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} inbound leads this period. MoM: {mom}%.

definition: Leads generated by outbound prospecting — SDR outreach, LinkedIn, cold email, events. Lower intent but scalable.
benchmark: Outbound:Inbound ratio should align with GTM motion (PLG vs sales-led)
formula: COUNT(contact_id) WHERE source IN ('Outbound SDR','LinkedIn Outbound','Events') AND created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} outbound leads. Cost per outbound lead: {cost}. Touch-to-MQL rate: {pct}%.

definition: Revenue or pipeline credited to the first marketing touchpoint that brought the contact in. Shows what creates awareness.
benchmark: Compare against last touch to understand full-funnel contribution
formula: SUM(deal amount) grouped by first source on contact record
insight_template: First-touch leader: {source} driving {value} pipeline. {pct}% of total.

definition: Revenue attributed to the last marketing interaction before conversion. Shows what closes or converts prospects.
benchmark: Typically favors sales-driven channels; balance with first touch for full picture
formula: SUM(deal amount) grouped by last activity source before deal creation
insight_template: Last-touch leader: {source} at {value}. Compare to first-touch: {delta}.

definition: Total marketing spend divided by total leads generated. Basic efficiency metric for demand generation spend.
benchmark: CPL varies widely by channel: Organic $5–$20, Paid $50–$200, Events $200–$500
formula: Marketing Spend / Total Leads Generated
insight_template: Blended CPL: {value}. Lowest cost source: {source} at {cost}.

definition: Marketing spend per marketing qualified lead. More meaningful than CPL as it filters for quality threshold.
benchmark: B2B SaaS: $150–$500 per MQL depending on ACV
formula: Marketing Spend / MQL Count
insight_template: Cost per MQL: {value}. {direction} vs target {target}.

definition: Spend required to generate one qualified sales opportunity. Directly tied to revenue ROI of marketing investment.
benchmark: Should be less than 20% of target ACV to maintain healthy unit economics
formula: Marketing Spend / Opportunities Created
insight_template: Cost per opportunity: {value}. Payback at current win rate: {months} months.

definition: Percentage of total pipeline where marketing was the originating source. Measures marketing's direct contribution to revenue.
benchmark: Marketing should source 30–50% of pipeline in a balanced GTM
formula: Pipeline from Marketing Sources / Total Pipeline × 100
insight_template: Marketing sourced {value}% of pipeline ({amount}). Target: {target}%.

definition: Percentage of pipeline where marketing had any touchpoint (not necessarily first). Broader than sourced — shows overall influence.
benchmark: Should be 60–80% in a mature marketing org
formula: COUNT(deals where contact had any marketing touch) / Total Deals × 100
insight_template: Marketing influenced {value}% of all pipeline. {n} deals have zero marketing touch.

definition: Distribution of leads/contacts across all acquisition channels. Used to assess diversification and rebalance spend.
benchmark: No channel should exceed 50% to avoid dependency risk
formula: GROUP BY source: COUNT(contact_id) / Total Contacts × 100
insight_template: Top channel: {source} at {pct}%. Diversification score: {score}/10.

definition: Total number of invoices issued in the period. Volume metric for billing team throughput.
benchmark: Should track with deal closures and renewal schedules
formula: COUNT(invoice_id) WHERE issued_date IN period
insight_template: {value} invoices raised this period. Total value: {amount}.

definition: Count and value of invoices that have been collected. Measures collections efficiency vs issued.
benchmark: Payment rate above 85% within 30 days is healthy
formula: COUNT(invoice_id) WHERE is_paid = True AND paid_date IN period
insight_template: {value} invoices paid. Collection rate: {pct}%. Total collected: {amount}.

definition: Value of unpaid invoices (Accounts Receivable). High AR means cash is trapped; poor collections affect runway.
benchmark: AR should not exceed 45 days of revenue. Track by aging bucket.
formula: SUM(net_amount) WHERE is_paid = False
insight_template: Outstanding AR: {value}. {overdue_pct}% is overdue ({overdue_amount}).

definition: Average days between invoice issuance and payment receipt. Lower DSO = faster cash collection.
benchmark: Net-30 terms: DSO below 35 days. Net-60: below 65 days.
formula: AVG(days_to_pay) WHERE is_paid = True
insight_template: DSO: {value} days. {direction} vs prior period. {n} invoices beyond 60 days.

definition: Percentage of unpaid invoices that have passed their due date. Indicates collections process gaps or customer cash flow issues.
benchmark: Below 10% of invoice volume overdue is acceptable. Above 25% needs escalation.
formula: COUNT(is_overdue = True) / COUNT(is_paid = False) × 100
insight_template: {value}% of open invoices are overdue. Total at risk: {amount}.

definition: Total invoice amount raised per month. Reveals billing seasonality, end-of-quarter spikes, and growth trajectory.
benchmark: Should trend upward with ARR growth; flat indicates billing process lag
formula: GROUP BY MONTH(issued_date): SUM(net_amount)
insight_template: Peak billing month: {month} at {value}. QoQ trend: {trend}.

definition: Mean value of invoices issued. Tracks whether average transaction size is growing (upsell success) or shrinking (discounting).
benchmark: Should grow in line with ACV growth; stagnant avg invoice amid ARR growth signals more smaller deals
formula: AVG(net_amount) FROM invoices WHERE issued_date IN period
insight_template: Avg invoice: {value}. {direction} vs prior period. Median: {median}.

definition: Average discount percentage applied to invoices. High discounting erodes margin and sets bad pricing precedent.
benchmark: Below 10% avg discount is healthy. Enterprise deals may justify 15–20%.
formula: AVG(discount_pct) WHERE discount_pct > 0
insight_template: Avg discount: {value}%. Revenue left on table: {amount} this period.

definition: Total value of refunds or credit notes issued to customers. High refunds signal product delivery issues or over-selling.
benchmark: Refund rate below 2% of revenue is acceptable; higher needs root cause review
formula: COUNT and SUM of invoices with status = 'Refunded' or credits issued
insight_template: {n} refunds issued totaling {value}. {pct}% of revenue. Top reason: {reason}.

definition: Percentage of invoiced revenue successfully collected in the period. End-to-end billing health metric.
benchmark: Above 90% within 60 days of invoice is strong
formula: SUM(paid invoices net_amount) / SUM(all invoices net_amount) × 100
insight_template: Collections rate: {value}%. {amount} outstanding across {n} invoices.

definition: Average number of calls recorded by each rep in the CRM. Activity volume is the leading indicator of pipeline creation.
benchmark: SDRs: 40–60 calls/day. AEs: 10–20 calls/day on top of meetings.
formula: COUNT(activity_count filtered by call type) grouped by owner_rep
insight_template: Top caller: {rep} at {n} calls. Team avg: {avg} calls/week.

definition: Volume of outbound emails sent per rep. Combined with reply rate gives a fuller picture of outreach effectiveness.
benchmark: Personalized: 20–40/day. Sequenced: 50–100/day with automation.
formula: COUNT(email activities) grouped by owner_rep in period
insight_template: Top emailer: {rep} at {n} emails. Reply rate avg: {reply}%.

definition: Count of meetings scheduled by the team. Key SDR output metric that bridges prospecting to pipeline creation.
benchmark: SDR: 5–10 meetings/week. AE: 3–6 meetings/week
formula: COUNT(meetings created) WHERE created_date IN period
insight_template: {value} meetings booked. Avg per rep: {avg}. Show rate: {show_rate}%.

definition: Meetings that were actually attended (not cancelled/no-show). Show rate = held/booked. Low show rate = qualification problem.
benchmark: Show rate above 70% is good. Below 50% indicates poor prospect quality or confirmation process.
formula: COUNT(meetings with outcome = held) WHERE date IN period
insight_template: {value} meetings held. Show rate: {show_rate}%. No-show impact: {cost}.

definition: Percentage of CRM tasks completed on time vs total tasks created. Measures rep discipline and CRM hygiene.
benchmark: Above 85% completion rate; overdue tasks signal workload issues or bad habits
formula: COUNT(tasks completed) / COUNT(tasks created) × 100
insight_template: Task completion: {value}%. {n} overdue tasks across {reps} reps.

definition: Average days between an activity occurring (call/meeting) and it being logged in the CRM. High lag = data unreliable for reporting.
benchmark: Same-day logging is best practice. Above 2 days is a data quality risk.
formula: AVG(log_date - activity_date) in days per rep
insight_template: Avg entry lag: {value} days. {rep} has highest lag at {days} days.

definition: Composite score combining calls, emails, meetings, and tasks per rep. Normalizes multi-channel activity into one health number.
benchmark: Score below 40/100 two weeks in a row triggers coaching conversation
formula: Weighted average of calls (30%) + emails (20%) + meetings (35%) + tasks (15%) normalized to 100
insight_template: Top performer: {rep} at {score}/100. {n} reps below threshold of 40.

definition: Average number of rep interactions per closed deal. More touches often mean more complex/enterprise deals.
benchmark: SMB: 5–10 touches. Enterprise: 20–50+ touches across multiple stakeholders
formula: AVG(activity_count) WHERE stage = 'Closed Won'
insight_template: Avg {value} touches per won deal. Deals below 5 touches win {pct}% less often.

definition: Percentage of new leads enrolled in an outreach sequence within 24 hours of creation. Measures SDR responsiveness.
benchmark: Above 80% enrollment within 24h is strong. Below 50% wastes inbound leads.
formula: COUNT(contacts enrolled in sequence within 24h) / COUNT(new contacts) × 100
insight_template: Enrollment rate: {value}%. {n} new leads with no sequence in 48h.

definition: Breakdown of why deals were won or lost. The most actionable insight for improving sales process, pricing, and product.
benchmark: Price should not be top loss reason if NPS is high — usually signals positioning gap
formula: GROUP BY win_reason / loss_reason: COUNT(deal_id) WHERE stage IN closed
insight_template: Top loss reason: {loss} at {loss_pct}%. Top win reason: {win} at {win_pct}%.
